title: Behdad Finding
description: A single typed, evidence-grounded audit finding. This is the interface passed between inspectors, the critic, and the manager. Also usable as a Codex output_schema.

id.description: Stable identifier for this finding. Deterministic hash of (aspect,file,line,canonical_ids) is preferred over random so re-runs can be diffed.

aspect.description: Which inspector domain produced this finding.

title.description: Short one-line summary of the issue.

canonical_ids.description: Canonical standard identifiers for traceability and dedup, e.g. CWE-89, OWASP-A03, ASVS-V5.3.4, WCAG-1.1.1.

risk_score.description: Blended risk = f(CVSS, EPSS, environmental) per config/severity.yaml. Used for ranking. 0 if not computed.

confidence.description: Calibrated confidence that this finding is a true positive. Findings that ABSTAIN set status=abstain and low confidence.

file.description: Repo-relative path of the file the finding anchors to.

line.description: 1-indexed line the finding anchors to. 0 if file-level / not line-specific.

evidence.description: Concrete evidence: the offending code snippet plus a specific explanation of why it is a problem. No hand-waving.

detected_by.description: Sources that flagged this: scanner rule IDs (e.g. semgrep:python.lang.security.audit), inspector name, and voter IDs.

agreement.description: Fraction of independent sources/voters that flagged this same finding. Drives consensus ranking.

status.description: candidate = raw inspector output; verified = survived the critic gate; rejected = killed by critic; abstain = inspector was unsure; suppressed = matched a learned suppression.

verification.description: How the critic tried to prove/refute this finding.

proposed_fix.description: Unified diff that would remediate the finding. Empty if no safe automated fix is available.

explanation_for_humans.description: Plain-language explanation of the risk aimed at developers who don't know what they don't know. Why it matters, what could go wrong.

ground_truth.description: True if this finding is anchored to a deterministic scanner result (highest trust); false if it is a pure-LLM reasoning finding (must clear the hardest verification).
